text1:
Suffering from patchy or thin eyebrows that affects your appearance and personality? Eyebrow hair transplant Turkey, a trending facial aesthetic procedure is the best option for you. The density and shape of your brows change the way you look, giving a softer or harsher appearance and affecting facial symmetry.

You might be experiencing hair loss due to conditions like alopecia areata, androgenic alopecia or improper trimming. Whatever the cause of poorly shaped brows, visit a trusted clinic like <color>Aslı Tarcan</color> in Turkey for consultation and eyebrow enhancement. Our professional experts will provide the best eyebrow transplant experience.

Let’s explore what is eyebrow transplant procedure, before and after care, and the eyebrow transplant cost in Turkey and other countries.
text2:
Eyebrow hair transplant is a cosmetic procedure where hair follicles (tiny hair-producing units) are collected from the back of your head, called the donor area, and transferred to the eyebrow area. On average, it requires about 300 grafts containing 1 to 4 hair follicles.

Patients with long hair can easily cover the donor area, resuming daily lives without any noticeable difference. The procedure is performed under anesthesia for maximum comfort, although non-invasive hair transplant options exist.
text3:
Get natural-looking eyebrows with permanent results in hair growth. Some key advantages of this procedure include:

• Get the desired brow hair density.
• Natural & permanent hair loss solution.
• Successful outcome after 6 months.
• High success rate of almost 100%.
• Safe treatment for enhancing facial aesthetics.
• Overall great change to your appearance.
• Self-confidence booster.
• No need for makeup to shape thinning brows.
• Customized eyebrow shape, tailored to your desire.
title3: Who Gets An Eyebrow Hair Transplant?
text4:
Eligible candidates for an eyebrow transplant include:

• Individuals older than 18.
• Seeking the procedure for cosmetic reasons.
• People with open areas on the eyebrows due to injuries.
• Individuals with eyebrow defects from burns (post-burn alopecia).
• Suffering from stabilized Alopecia areata, an autoimmune condition causing hair loss.
• People with Hypothyroidism, experiencing eyebrows or eyelashes loss.
• Having a hair-pulling disorder, called trichotillomania.
• Hair loss triggered by Hanson's disease (leprosy).
text5:
Before an eyebrow transplant, take care of the following pre-requisites for a successful treatment:

• Share your expectations and history with the surgeon.
• Stop herbal supplements (e.g., ginkgo biloba, ginseng, fish oil) 1 week before.
• Have someone available to drive you home post-surgery.
• Avoid alcohol for 3 days before the procedure.
• Consult a doctor regarding regular medication use.
• Discuss side effects, anesthesia choice, and recovery details with your doctor.
text6: The general eyebrow transplant procedure remains the same for all kinds of cases, with minor changes in the tools used. Most commonly used is the FUE (Follicular unit extraction) method, a minimally invasive procedure requiring no scalpels and stitches. The general step-by-step procedure is given below: Step 1: Consultation and Diagnosis You can consult our doctors about your future expectations regarding eyebrow hair transplant. Your medical history will be reviewed here, along with pre-operative blood sampling, to rule out any risk of disease. The preparatory markings are made for the new position of the eyebrows. Step 2: Follicle Extraction Local anesthetic drugs are injected to numb the eyebrow area and hair follicles are collected from the nape area using fine-tipped tools. The lower back neck is typically the donor area of choice as it is softer with inherently shorter hair. Step 3: Implantation Techniques A side slit will open the microchannels in the brow area. The extracted hair follicles are stitched one by one into the open channels at appropriate angles. The no touch-to-root approach is used to reduce the risk of damaging the follicles. Traditional FUE uses steel blades, while the Sapphire FUE procedure uses Sapphire blades for their hardness and sharpness. Alternatively, the DHI method uses a special tool named Choi implanter pen for extraction and implantation. It also offers superior results in brow shaping with the least discomfort and scarring. Step 4: Aftercare & Recovery The surgery typically gets completed within one session and usually takes around 4 to 6 hours. The donor area is dressed for 2 days to better heal it, while the brow area is left uncovered. Patients usually stay in the clinic for the remainder of the day and are dismissed when it is ensured no risk of complications exists. Hair regrowth begins at 3–4 months and increases after 4–6 months. You can see a gradual, visible improvement after 6–12 months.
text7:
Proper aftercare is essential for optimal outcomes from the best eyebrow transplant Turkey. Some post-procedure care elements include:

• Get plenty of rest after surgery.
• Wear bandages as advised.
• Take prescribed medicines for 3–5 days.
• Avoid direct sunlight for 10 days, then use of 50+ SPF sunscreen.
• Refrain from strenuous activities for 3 weeks.
• Apply hair gel 3 weeks post-procedure to train hair direction.
• Keep your head elevated while sleeping to avoid graft contact with the pillow.
• Quit smoking and drinking for at least 10 days.
• Avoid swimming for 4 weeks.
• Be gentle while washing surgical site(s).
• Make follow-up appointments to monitor healing.
text8:
As with any cosmetic procedure, eyebrow transplant surgery carries potential risks, including:

• Loss of feeling or hair follicles at the surgical site.
• Poor regrowth requiring a touch-up procedure.
• Graft removal due to poor hair growth angulation.
• Inclusion cysts (rarely).
• Misaligned eyebrows due to anesthesia positioning.
• Hair growing in different directions or textures.
• Patchy hair growth if transplants do not take.
• Rare complications such as infection, scarring, folliculitis, bruising, or swelling.
text10: Do eyebrow transplants work? Yes, eyebrow transplant is a successful type of cosmetic surgery to improve the fullness of your eyebrows. Is an eyebrow transplant painful? An eyebrow transplant isn’t painful because you’ll receive general anesthesia, so you won’t feel anything during the surgery. You may experience mild pain as your skin heals after the procedure. Do eyebrows grow long after an eyebrow transplant? As the hair for your transplant is from your scalp, it’ll continue to grow as it would if it were still on your scalp, even though it’s now on your eyebrow. You may need to regularly trim your new eyebrows to keep the hair at the length you want it to be. When Will I See the Results? It takes 6 to 8 months for transplanted hair to grow, while the full results can be expected within a year. How long does an eyebrow transplant last? An eyebrow transplant is a long-term solution. Your results can last a lifetime. What’s the difference between an eyebrow transplant and micro-blading? Although both are cosmetic procedures to make your eyebrows look fuller. Micro-blading is a semi-permanent type of cosmetic tattoo that can last for a few years. In contrast, an eyebrow transplant is a permanent solution that moves hair follicles from your scalp to your eyebrows. What is the difference between sapphire FUE and DHI procedures? In a Sapphire FUE procedure, incisions (canals) and implantation are two different phases of the surgery. In a DHI procedure, implantation and incisions are made at the same time with the implanter pens. Can any scar be left in the donor area after the transplant procedure? If it is not done correctly, yes, there can be scarring in your donor area, especially if you require a high number of grafts. So, go to a reliable, experienced eyebrow transplant clinic like Asli Tarcan in Turkey.
